kit.landingCopy:
Headline: Streaky — habit tracking, local-first.
Subhead: Privacy-first habit tracker built for indie developers. Your data stays on your machine. No account. No cloud dependency. No subscription.
Features: Local SQLite store · AES-256 encrypted export · CLI + lightweight web UI · Zero telemetry · Self-hostable sync.
CTA: Install now — npm install -g streaky — MIT licensed, open source.
tweetThread: Most habit trackers demand an account, a subscription, and your personal data. We built one that doesn't. Introducing Streaky: a privacy-first habit tracker for indie devs. 🧵
100% local-first. Your data lives in a local SQLite store on your machine. No cloud sync, no mandatory accounts, and absolutely zero telemetry. You own your data.
Built for the terminal. Get a minimal, keyboard-centric CLI plus a lightweight web UI. Tick off habits without leaving your workflow or reaching for your mouse.
Fully transparent and open-source under MIT. Need sync? Use the optional, self-hostable sync server. Export your data anytime with AES-256 encrypted backups.
Stop paying SaaS subscriptions to gamify your life. Install it now and build better habits on your own terms: npm install -g streaky
kit.shortPitch: Build better habits without surrendering your data to the cloud. Local-first, open-source, zero telemetry.
kit.phHnBlurb: Hey everyone, we were frustrated with habit trackers that require cloud accounts and gamify streaks into guilt trips. So we built Streaky. It's a privacy-first, offline-first habit tracker specifically for developers. Your data is stored 100% locally in SQLite with zero telemetry. We use a minimal, keyboard-centric CLI alongside a lightweight web UI so you can log habits without breaking your flow. It's MIT licensed and fully open-source. There are no accounts, no subscriptions, and no mandatory cloud dependency. If you want to sync across machines, you can run the optional self-hostable sync server. We also support AES-256 encrypted exports. We'd love your feedback on the architecture and the CLI UX.
kit.readmePolish:
# Streaky — habit tracking, local-first

**Streaky** is a privacy-first habit tracker built for indie developers. Your data stays on your machine. No account. No cloud dependency. No subscription.

## Why Streaky?

Most habit trackers are cloud-dependent SaaS products that track you as much as you track your habits. Streaky is different. It is offline-first and relies on a 100% local SQLite store, guaranteeing that your personal data never leaves your machine unless you explicitly want it to.

- **Local-first:** Data stored entirely in SQLite. Zero telemetry.
- **Open Source:** MIT licensed. Transparent, auditable codebase.
- **Keyboard-centric:** Minimal CLI + lightweight web UI.
- **Secure:** AES-256 encrypted exports and optional self-hostable sync.

## Installation

```bash
npm install -g streaky
```

data.oneLiner: Build better habits without surrendering your data to the cloud.

requirements.brief: Research the market for a privacy-first habit tracker app targeting indie developers. Key angles: (1) Competitive landscape — existing habit trackers (Habitica, Streaks, Loop, etc.) and their privacy/data practices. (2) Audience pain points — indie developers who value data sovereignty, local-first storage, and open-source transparency. (3) Positioning opportunities — how to differentiate on privacy (no cloud, no telemetry, local encryption, open-source). (4) Pricing benchmarks for similar indie/privacy-focused apps. (5) Key messaging hooks that resonate with a technical, privacy-conscious audience. Tone: technical, straightforward, and privacy-conscious.
